blocks.heading: A presentation is a set of slides. 🖥️
blocks.html:
In Year 11 you learned to make slides with text and pictures.

In Year 12 you learn how to make slides move and link to other places.

blocks.heading: A transition is the change between two slides. 🔄
blocks.html:
It happens when one slide ends and the next slide starts.

Example: the old slide fades away and the new slide fades in.

blocks.heading: Animation moves things on ONE slide. ✨
blocks.html:
A transition changes between slides.

An animation moves an object, like text or a picture, while you are still on the same slide.

blocks.heading: Use transitions and animations with care. ⚠️
blocks.html:
Too many effects can be distracting.

The audience should focus on your message, not on flashy effects.

blocks.heading: A hyperlink is a clickable link. 🔗
blocks.html:
Click it to jump to another slide, a website, or a file.

Hyperlinks help the audience move around quickly.

blocks.heading: Print preview shows your slides before printing. 🖨️
blocks.html:
It lets you check the layout looks right.

You can choose to print slides, handouts, or notes pages.

blocks.title: Match each word to its meaning. 🧩

pairs.definition: Movement of an object on one slide ✨
A clickable link to another place 🔗
Shows slides before you print them 🖨️

options: Movement of text on one slide
question.explanation: A transition is the change you see when moving from one slide to the next slide.

options: Moves an object on one slide
question.explanation: Animation moves an object, like a picture or text box, while you stay on the same slide.

question.text: Why should you use effects with care? ⚠️
options: They can distract the audience from your message
question.explanation: Too many transitions and animations distract the audience. Your message should always come first.

blocks.heading: Elements of design are the basic building blocks of a slide. 🧱
blocks.html:
There are four elements you need to know:

Line, shape, space, and colour.

blocks.heading: A line connects or separates things. ➖
blocks.html:
Lines can guide the eye around the slide.

Example: a line under a heading separates it from the body text.

blocks.heading: A shape is a closed area, like a circle or box. ⬛
blocks.html:
Shapes can hold text or pictures.

Shapes can also group related items together.

blocks.heading: Space is the empty area on a slide. 🌌
blocks.html:
It is also called white space.

Empty space stops a slide from looking too busy and crowded.

blocks.heading: Colour sets the mood and draws attention. 🎨
blocks.html:
Bright colours grab attention fast.

Calm colours, like blue or grey, feel more relaxed and professional.

blocks.heading: Use colour the same way on every slide. 🌈
blocks.html:
This keeps your presentation looking neat.

Example: always use the same colour for headings.

blocks.title: Match each element of design to its meaning. 🧩

pairs.definition: Connects or separates things, and guides the eye ➖
A closed area, like a box or circle ⬛
The empty area on a slide 🌌
Sets the mood and draws attention 🎨

question.text: What is another name for space in design? 🌌
question.explanation: Empty space on a slide is called white space. It stops the slide from looking too crowded.

question.text: What is a shape used for in design? ⬛
options: To guide the eye between two points
To hold text or pictures, or group items
To remove colour from a slide
question.explanation: A shape is a closed area. It can hold text or pictures, or group related items together.

question.text: Which of these is one of the four elements of design? ➖
question.explanation: The four elements of design are line, shape, space, and colour. Balance and unity are principles of design, not elements.

blocks.heading: Principles of design are the RULES for using the elements. 📐
blocks.html:
Elements are the building blocks (line, shape, space, colour).

Principles are the rules for arranging them well: balance, emphasis, and unity.

blocks.heading: Balance means items are spread out evenly. ⚖️
blocks.html:
A balanced slide does not feel heavy on one side.

Example: do not put all your pictures on the left and leave the right empty.

blocks.heading: See the difference between balanced and unbalanced. ⚖️
blocks.html: The left slide is unbalanced. The right slide is balanced.<svg viewBox="0 0 280 130" style="display:block;width:100%;margin-top:14px;border-radius:8px" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="280" height="130" fill="#f8fafc" rx="6" stroke="#e2e8f0" stroke-width="1"/><rect x="8" y="8" width="122" height="114" rx="4" fill="white" stroke="#fca5a5" stroke-width="1.5"/><circle cx="35" cy="30" r="12" fill="#fca5a5"/><circle cx="60" cy="30" r="12" fill="#fca5a5"/><circle cx="35" cy="58" r="12" fill="#fca5a5"/><text x="69" y="108" font-family="Arial,sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="700" fill="#dc2626" text-anchor="middle">Unbalanced</text><rect x="150" y="8" width="122" height="114" rx="4" fill="white" stroke="#86efac" stroke-width="1.5"/><circle cx="178" cy="30" r="12" fill="#86efac"/><circle cx="244" cy="30" r="12" fill="#86efac"/><circle cx="178" cy="95" r="12" fill="#86efac"/><circle cx="244" cy="95" r="12" fill="#86efac"/><text x="211" y="108" font-family="Arial,sans-serif" font-size="11" font-weight="700" fill="#16a34a" text-anchor="middle">Balanced</text></svg>

blocks.heading: Emphasis makes one thing stand out. ⭐
blocks.html:
Emphasis has two parts: contrast and proportion.

These both help the most important thing get noticed first.

blocks.heading: Contrast uses different colours to stand out. 🌓
blocks.html:
Example: dark text on a light background.

High contrast is easy to read from across a room.

blocks.heading: Proportion is the SIZE of one thing compared to another. 📏
blocks.html:
This is a new word for Year 12.

Example: make your title BIG and your footnote small. The size difference shows what matters most.

blocks.heading: Unity means everything looks like it belongs together. 🧩
blocks.html:
This is also a new word for Year 12.

A presentation has unity when every slide uses the same fonts, colours, and style.

blocks.heading: Unity vs balance — know the difference. 🔍
blocks.html:
Balance is about ONE slide looking even.

Unity is about ALL slides looking like they belong to the same set.

blocks.title: Match each principle of design to its meaning. 🧩

pairs.definition: Items are spread out evenly on a slide ⚖️
Different colours make something stand out 🌓
The size of one thing compared to another 📏
All slides look like they belong together 🧩

blocks.title: Fill in the blank: Making your title bigger than your footnote is an example of ___.
blocks.explanation: Proportion is the size of one thing compared to another. A bigger title shows it is more important than a small footnote.

question.text: What does balance mean in design? ⚖️
options: One slide uses many bright colours
question.explanation: Balance means items on a slide are spread out evenly. The slide should not feel heavy on one side.

question.text: Emphasis is made up of which two things? ⭐
question.explanation: Emphasis has two parts: contrast (different colours) and proportion (different sizes). Both help one thing stand out.

question.text: A presentation where every slide uses the same fonts and colours has good... 🧩
question.explanation: Unity means all slides look like they belong together. Using the same fonts and colours creates unity.
